title: nonnull parameters and return values of returns_nonnull functions should not be null
description: What is the potential impact? A function’s return value and parameters may be decorated with attributes to convey additional information to the compiler and/or other developers. A commonly used attribute is nonnull which can be used to mark a function’s return value and parameters as shown in the following: __attribute__((returns_nonnull)) int * make_array_copy(int *src, size_t len) __attribute__((nonnull(1))); int *make_array_copy(int *src, size_t len) { int *dst = (int *)malloc(len * sizeof(int)); perror("malloc failed"); memcpy(dst, src, len); The nonnull attribute is meant for other developers and as a hint for compilers. Values marked as nonnull are assumed to have non-null values.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: Nonnull parameters and return values of returns_nonnull functions should not be null. Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.
